product.title: Garmin Alpha 300i Handheld, Advanced Tracking and Training Handheld with inReach® Technology
product.description: Lead your pack with the Alpha® 300i dog tracking and training handheld. It features inReach® satellite technology for two-way messaging and interactive SOS alerts (active subscription required; jurisdiction restrictions may apply). Track and train up to 20 dogs from up to 9 miles when paired with your compatible dog collars. See it all on a vivid 3.5” touchscreen display with up to a 2.5-second update rate. Easily control training functions using the button-operated design, which gives you 18 levels of stim, tone or vibration when paired to your training-enabled collar. Access premium Garmin navigation features, such as preloaded TopoActive maps, satellite imagery, and a built-in 3-axis compass and barometric altimeter. With up to 50 hours of battery life, you don’t have to worry about running out of power before your dogs do.

attributes.name: Included Components
attributes.value: Alpha 300i handheld with rechargeable lithium-ion battery pack, belt clip, AC adapter, USB-C cable, documentation

feature_bullets: Features a vivid sunlight-readable 3.5” capacitive touchscreen display and a button-operated, dog-focused design for quick, minor adjustments
Easily set up and monitor up to 20 dogs with your compatible dog collars from up to 9 miles away (depending on your dog collar) with up to a 2.5-second update rate

With an Outdoor Maps+ subscription, you have access to a wide variety of premium mapping content that is regularly updated and wirelessly delivered directly to your Alpha 300i handheld when connected to a wireless network
Dog list and group management let you keep non-paired dog information in the handheld for easier setup

Send interactive SOS alerts and two-way messaging via the 100% global Iridium satellite network with inReach satellite technology (active subscription required; some jurisdictions regulate or prohibit the use of satellite communication devices; it is the responsibility of the user to know and follow all applicable laws in the jurisdictions where the device is intended to be used)
Communicate directly with Garmin ResponseSM [global search and rescue coordination center] when there’s an emergency
Includes preloaded TopoActive maps, downloadable satellite imagery, and a built-in 3-axis compass and barometric altimeter
User-replaceable battery lasts up to 55 hours; up to 50 hours with inReach satellite technology enabled
Triggers 18 levels of stim, tone or vibration when paired with your training-enabled collar
Backward compatible with select existing collars and other Alpha handheld-compatible devices; transfer location data between compatible Garmin devices

products.title: Garmin Alpha 300i Handheld, Advanced Tracking and Training Handheld with inReach® Technology
Garmin Alpha TT 25 GPS Dog Tracking and Training Collar
Supershieldz (2 Pack) Designed for Garmin Alpha 300 300i Tempered Glass Screen Protector, Anti Scratch, Bubble Free

local.title: Reliable, long battery life
local.text: This is a high quality piece of equipment. It connects with a TT25 in a few seconds, the connection is solid, and it pretty much works well in all conditions. I am using it 5 times a week, 1h to 1h30 each time, and I charge both this unit and the TT25 every other week. Not by absolute necessity, but just because the batteries are at or just below 50%.The software is showing its age. The UI could be improved, in the age of iPhones, but it works. I guess there is a tradeoff between usability and battery life. This one is prioritizing battery life. I only had one crash in 6 months.I like the physical buttons to activate the collar. Unfortunately they do not work when the screen went to sleep after a while, so you first have to wake up the screen to use these buttons. That would be the one thing to fix if I had to pick one.Great unit though. Maps are preloaded and are very usable. It’s proven software and high grade hardware.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 6, 2025

local.title: Solid unit
local.text: Works as it should. Great buy.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 8, 2026

local.title: works, reasonable monthly rate.
local.text: i got this, believe it or not, along with a dog collar for my dog who will not eliminate on lead. we were vacationing in the wilds of nh, and i didn't want to have to worry about her running off never to be seen again. she's well behaved overall, but can be skittish in a new environment. this unit works in areas with no cell reception and gave us a low stress vacation.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 18, 2025

local.title: Details accurate
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 9, 2026

local.title: Way better than the Alpha 100. Good for hiking.
local.text: So, I bought both the Alpha 100 and the Alpha 300i for comparison. The 300i is way more intuitive and easy to use - hands down. The bigger screen is a must for my big thumbs to navigate. It's a big price difference, but I couldn't go back to the Alpha 100 after trying the Alpha 300i. I bought this to track my dogs while hiking. It doubles as a standalone backpacking GPS too. I'm pleased.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 6, 2024

local.title: Best Alpha Yet!!
local.text: Fast shipping and great device. I did upgrade and buy another map after receiving though.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 13, 2023

local.title: Great gps

local.title: Do not buy!
local.text: Will not connect with the tt25, which it was specifically designed for to connect to. I've tried factory resetting both the tt25 and the Alpha 300i, I've updated both to the latest firmware, I've tried pairing away from other devices such as phones, other wifi signals, etc. This product does not work.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 20, 2025
